ab: apiya o kapacici ko jimo?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: jiapiya, Ko apiyaen,
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: apiya o kapacici ko jimo?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: taosya, ta yapereh ko katenngan.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: yamya so vazey mo simakoyab? ko ikakza ko kangey jimo a pacicien.
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: jiapiya, nanalahen ko imo do vahey namen.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: yamya so vazey mo simarow? ko pacicien imo do kavavatanen no tao.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於達悟族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I'd like to ask you about the legendary story of the Tao tribe.

ab: ko mangey do takey simarow. ori ko jingyani do vahey namen.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: ko mammyamizing am, yaro ko no mo katenngan a iwawalam no tao.ipanci mo jiaken an.
ch: A:我聽說您對達悟族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Tao tribe, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: yapereh ko katenngan, o katenngan ko o ononongan ko an.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: apiya o ka siva ko jimo? ta jiko ngawawi niya.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: jiapiya, ikongo salit na.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: yapira o kakawakawan mo?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: yasyam a ngarnan rana o kakawakawan ko.
en: B: I'm 90 years old.

ab: ka malavayo pa.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: tomancian! ko na rarakeh.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: do anjin mo nikapoan?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: ko nimapo do Jiraraley.
en: B: I'm from Long Island Village.

ab: tao do pongso o mavakes mo?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是達悟族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife a member of the Tao tribe?

ab: Beken, ta bonong o mavakes ko.
en: B: No, she's a Bunun.

ab: sino mo katenngan so ciriciring?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: ko katenngan o ciriciring da no amis kano peynan.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak [Aramaic] and [Puyuma].

ab: ko masarey a yamakamizing so mo ipanci, pancian pala o makanyow do among no rayon?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關飛魚禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you tell us about the flying fish taboo?

ab: yapiya. no kalavayo ko pa am, masanib ko a manawaz so alibangbang.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常捕撈飛魚。
en: B: Yes. When I was young, I used to catch a lot of flying fish.

ab: ayoy mo ninanao jiaken sicyarow ya. ya teyapiya.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: yabo do dang o yamalas a ko nipanci. a vahavahangsowen da ononongan no ineynapo namen.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，我們祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of our ancestors have been handed down.

ab: maminda do jia o pacician ko jimo sicyarow ya. ayoy mo nipanci.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: mo kamoan niyaken. no ipisa am, malavat ta.
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: mangey ko rana an, inaoy no aroaro pa o mamizing ko sipisa kai ko.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: noon! makapya ka an.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
